2606.26728.title: Scientific discovery as meta-optimization: a combinatorial optimization case study
2606.26728.paper_summary: Scientific discovery is fundamentally an optimization problem, defined by a vast "state space" of theories and experiments, and an evaluation criterion based on quality, novelty, and validity. Large language models (LLMs) have enabled automated exploration of this space, but we argue that simultaneous modification of the evaluation criteria is equally important. Here, we propose formalizing research as meta-optimization, where the optimization objective itself is also being optimized. Our key contribution is "consensus objective aggregation," where LLM-generated objective functions are combined via correlation-weighted voting, yielding a stable, self-correcting evaluation criterion that evolves as understanding deepens. We apply this framework to algorithm discovery for 3-SAT problems based on digital MemComputing machines, reducing the baseline scaling with problem size $N$ from $\sim N^{2.51}$ to $\sim N^{1.33}$ and delivering a $\sim 67\times$ speedup on the largest instances tested. As a problem-agnostic framework, we hope this approach will considerably aid scientific discovery.
2606.26728.paper_summary_zh: 科學發現本質上是一個優化問題，由廣泛的理論和實驗“狀態空間”定義，並基於質量、新穎性和有效性的評估標準。大型語言模型（LLMs）使得自動探索這一空間成為可能，但我們認為同時修改評估標準同樣重要。在這裡，我們提出將研究形式化為元優化，其中優化目標本身也在被優化。我們的主要貢獻是“共識目標聚合”，其中LLM生成的目標函數通過相關加權投票進行組合，產生一個穩定的、自我修正的評估標準，隨著理解的加深而演變。我們將這一框架應用於基於數字MemComputing機器的3-SAT問題的算法發現，將基線隨問題大小$N$的縮放從$\sim N^{2.51}$降低到$\sim N^{1.33}$，並在測試的最大實例上實現了約$\sim 67\times$的加速。作為一個問題無關的框架，我們希望這種方法能大大促進科學發現。
2606.26728.author: Yuan-Hang Zhang et.al.
2606.26728.authors: Yuan-Hang Zhang, Chesson Sipling, Massimiliano Di Ventra
